* Languages: Italian, English C1–C2

#### **Your Responsibilities**

  

* Internal accounting cycle management:  

collection, classification and submission of documentation to the accountant, handling the official bookkeeping and tax reporting.
* Accounts payable cycle:  

verification and approval of relevant supplier invoices; coordination with the accountant for those under their remit; execution of supplier payments due via online banking; execution of salary payments for the Italian team, in coordination with the employment consultant
* Company liquidity monitoring:  

periodic review of balances, short\-term cash flow forecasting and proactive reporting to management
* Corporate compliance:  

maintenance of statutory books, shareholder register, document archiving and filings with the Chamber of Commerce (company searches, certificates, updates to the business register)
* Expense management and reimbursements via Circula
* Personnel management:  

support to the People team on any Italy\-specific contractual or bureaucratic requirements, including local compliance and regulatory filings (such as mandatory employment notification to the Italian labour authorities \- UNILAV), health and safety obligations, and coordination of tool access and equipment setup for new hires.
* Office operations:  

lease agreement and landlord relations, procurement and maintenance of office supplies and equipment

**Plus Responsibilities — progressively over time**  

* Compliance and deadlines: tracking of corporate and regulatory deadlines
* Administrative management of contracts with professional service providers (accountant, employment consultant, other partners) such as filing, deadline and renewal monitoring, document collection
* Administrative correspondence with public authorities and suppliers (Italian and English)
* Administrative documentation: support for the parent company

#### **Your Profile**


Must\-haves

  

* At least 3 years of administrative experience in an SME, startup or professional firm (accountant / employment consultant)
* Experience as the sole or primary administrative reference in a small organisation
* Operational autonomy: able to organise their own work without daily supervision
* Practical knowledge of invoicing, VAT, F24 tax payments and basic accounting processes
* Fluent written English — communications with the parent company are in English
* Good command of Excel and cloud tools (Google Workspace or Microsoft 365\)


Nice to have  

* Diploma in Accountancy or degree in Economics
* Experience with accounting software
* Familiarity with international environments and reporting to a foreign parent company

#### **A Short Note from Your Future Lead**


Hi I’m Giacomo,  

  

and together with Michele I co\-lead Entrix Italy, where we focus on building and scaling our presence in one of Europe’s most exciting emerging battery storage markets. My role sits at the intersection of strategy, operations, and growth, helping shape how we establish Entrix as a leading player in Italy.  

  

Before joining Entrix, I spent more than seven years working across strategy consulting, private equity, and climate\-tech, with experience spanning a start\-up consultancy, a French private equity fund, and McKinsey \& Company. Throughout my career, I have focused on growth transformation, market expansion, and strategic development in sectors undergoing rapid change.  

  

I joined Entrix because I believe in what we are creating: a truly technology\-driven platform that unlocks the full value of battery storage across European energy markets. Italy represents a particularly compelling opportunity, with significant momentum still ahead and the chance to help shape a market at an early stage.  

  

We are building an ambitious team to help drive this next phase of growth and are always excited to meet people who bring ownership, strong judgement, and a collaborative mindset.  

If our mission resonates with you, we look forward to hearing from you.  

  

Best,  

Giacomo Lapo Ghezzi /Leading Business Operations

#### **Your Contact in our People Team**


Your contact person is Nagihan Bal (Talent Acquisition)  

If you have any questions, feel free to send us an email to talent@entrixenergy.com.  

We look forward to receiving your application!

#### **About us**

#### **We are an energy trader and market leader for trading flexible energy systems in Europe.**

  

Beyond AI\-powered trading, Entrix provides innovative solutions for long\-term revenue security and grid\-supportive battery operation. With our full\-service package, we support customers throughout every project phase – from technical design and integration, to continuous optimization, all the way to 24/7 operational support. That includes adapting to changing market conditions and unlocking new revenue opportunities.  

  

With more than 2\.3 GW and 38 battery storage assets under contract, Entrix is active in Germany, Poland, Italy, Spain, and Portugal, with offices in Munich, Warsaw, Milan, and Madrid. Since 2022, Entrix has been pioneering battery optimization in Germany by managing one of the country’s first large\-scale storage projects – and is now the trusted partner of leading infrastructure players such as Encavis, MEAG (Munich Re), as well as a wide range of local utilities.

title: Senior/ Executive Medical Director - Rheumatology, EMEA (client-dedicated)
description:
**Work Schedule**


Standard (Mon\-Fri)**Environmental Conditions**


Office**Job Description**


At Thermo Fisher Scientific, you’ll discover meaningful work that makes a positive impact on a global scale. Join our colleagues in bringing our Mission to life \- enabling our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. We provide our teams with the resources needed to achieve individual career goals while taking science a step beyond through research, development and delivery of life\-changing therapies. With clinical trials conducted in 100\+ countries and ongoing development of novel frameworks for clinical research through our PPD clinical research portfolio, our work spans laboratory, digital and decentralized clinical trial services. Your determination to deliver quality and accuracy will improve health outcomes that people and communities depend on – now and in the future.

**Discover Impactful Work:**


This position provides strategic leadership and tactical oversight for the clinical/medical aspects of all phases of the clinical drug development process. This person is responsible for the design, development and execution of Clinical Development Plans (CDPs), clinical trials and protocols in accordance with applicable GCP regulations. This person leads and contributes to the analysis of study results, the assessment of the extent to which the study(ies) has achieved clinical/regulatory objectives and the documentation of study results.


This person may act as a clinical/medical representative in meetings with external stakeholders (e.g. collaborators, Key Opinion Leaders (KOLs), investigators, regulatory authorities, etc.), partners (e.g. vendors, consultants, etc.) and other external audiences as needed.

**A day in the Life:**

* Provides medical content and leadership of the clinical studies
* Acts as therapeutic lead
* Medical Monitoring of clinical studies
* Establish and approve scientific methods underlying the design and implementation of clinical protocols
* Ensure study subject safety
* Regularly review adverse event, laboratory and other clinical data, ensure reporting is done on a timely basis
* Collaborate with the study executive and independent safety committee when needed
* Represent clinical research through membership on trial teams.
* Contributes to, reviews and approves study protocols, protocol amendments, study manuals, Investigator Brochures (IBs), Clinical Study Reports (CSRs), Informed Consent Forms (ICFs), Case Report Forms (CRFs) and other clinical documents as required for the conduct of clinical trials
* Interprets, summarizes and documents clinical data for regulatory documents (e.g. INDs, Annual Reports, NDAs, MAAs, etc.), manuscripts, and other publications as required.
* Collaborate with leading academic medical centers
* Recruit, guide and motivate clinical investigators
* Ensure study compliance by understanding and applying all relevant SOPs and GCPs
* Participate in long range strategic planning
* Line management responsibilities

**Education**

* MD degree, a minimum of 5 years of clinical drug development experience
* Drug Development experience in the area of Rheumatology

**Knowledge, Skills, Abilities**

* Demonstration and commitment to vigilant proactive problem solving
* Excellent leadership abilities
* Able to provide multiple examples of creating and implementing the scientific and clinical principles used for the preparation of clinical studies and clinical trial protocols.
* Demonstrated expertise in clinical trial design, conduct and interpretation of clinical data.
* Previous experience with regulatory submissions.
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
* Ability to travel.


Our Mission is to enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. Watch as our colleagues explain 5 reasons to work with us. As one team of 100,000\+ colleagues, we share a common set of values \- Integrity, Intensity, Innovation and Involvement \- working together to accelerate research, solve complex scientific challenges, drive technological innovation and support patients in need. \#StartYourStory with PPD, part of Thermo Fisher Scientific, where diverse experiences, backgrounds and perspectives are valued.

**Apply today! http://jobs.thermofisher.com**


Thermo Fisher Scientific is an EEO/Affirmative Action Employer and does not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, protected veteran status, disability or any other legally protected status.

title: Forwarding Management Professional Italy (Pantigliate)
description:
578413
  
  

Milano, IT
  
  

Forwarding Management Professional Italy (Pantigliate)
  
  

CEVA Logistics provides global supply chain solutions to connect people, products, and providers all around the world. Present in 170\+ countries and with more than 110,000 employees spread over 1,500 sites, we are proud to be a Top 5 global 3PL.
  
  

We believe that our employees are the key to our success. We want to engage and empower our diverse, global team to co\-create value with our customers through our solutions in contract logistics and air, ocean, ground, and finished vehicle transport. That is why CEVA Logistics offers a dynamic and exceptional work environment that fosters personal growth, innovation, and continuous improvement.
  
  

DARE TO GROW! Join CEVA Logistics, and you will be part of a team that values imagination and continued learning and is committed to excellence in everything we do. Join us in our mission to shape the future of global logistics. As we continue growing at a fast pace, will you “Dare to Grow” with us?
  
  

Forwarding Management Professional Italy (Pantigliate) \- CEVA Air \& Ocean Italy S.P.A.
  
  

CEVA Air \& Ocean Italy S.P.A., a leading company within the logistics industry, is seeking an experienced and dedicated professional to join our Ocean Operations team as a Forwarding Management Professional based in Milano/Pantigliate, Strada Vecchia Paullese. This is an excellent opportunity to contribute to the efficiency and growth of our Ocean division within the logistics sector, handling both air and ocean freight under the Logistics \- Air and Ocean SBU.
  
  

About the Role
  
  

As a Forwarding Management Professional, you will play a pivotal role in coordinating and managing forwarding operations to ensure seamless transport of goods. You will be responsible for overseeing shipments, managing customer relationships, and liaising with various stakeholders to guarantee timely and compliant delivery solutions. You will work in close collaboration with internal teams and external partners, supporting CEVA’s commitment to operational excellence and customer satisfaction.
  
  

Key Responsibilities
  
Manage end\-to\-end forwarding operations within the Ocean division, ensuring efficient coordination of shipments and compliance with regulatory requirements.
  
Collaborate with commercial teams and customers to provide tailored forwarding solutions aligned with client needs and company standards.
  
Oversee documentation processes, customs clearance, and compliance with international trade regulations.
  
Continuously monitor shipment progress, proactively identifying and resolving operational issues impacting delivery timelines.
  
Maintain strong relationships with carriers, agents, and other logistics partners to optimize service performance and cost\-efficiency.
  
Support process improvement initiatives by providing insights from operational data and industry trends.
  
Work within CEVA’s organisational frameworks and actively contribute to the Ocean Operations unit’s strategic objectives.
  
Candidate Profile
  
  

We are looking for a professional with a solid background in freight forwarding and logistics, particularly within the ocean freight sector. The ideal candidate will have a proven track record of managing complex shipping operations while demonstrating attention to detail, problem\-solving abilities, and strong communication skills.
  
  

Significant experience in air and ocean freight forwarding, with strong knowledge of logistics processes and maritime transport regulations.
  
Ability to manage multiple tasks, prioritise effectively, and work under pressure in a dynamic environment.
  
Excellent interpersonal skills to engage with diverse stakeholders, including customers, carriers, and internal teams.
  
Strong organisational and documentation management skills to ensure compliance with legal and company standards.
  
Fluency in English and Italian is essential to operate effectively within the local and international context.
  
Why Join CEVA Air \& Ocean Italy S.P.A.?
  
  

Joining CEVA means becoming part of a global logistics leader committed to innovation, sustainability, and professional growth. At CEVA, your expertise will have a direct impact on global supply chains, enabling businesses to move forward seamlessly. Our supportive environment encourages continuous learning, collaboration, and performance excellence.
  
  

We value diversity and inclusion and welcome applications from candidates of all backgrounds. Together, we create a workplace where everyone can thrive professionally and personally.
  
  

Location
  
  

This position is based in our Milano/Pantigliate office located on Strada Vecchia Paullese, offering a modern and well\-equipped work environment with convenient access to key transport networks.
  
  

How to Apply
  
  

If you are passionate about forwarding management and want to contribute to a dynamic and forward\-thinking logistics company, we encourage you to apply. Take the next step in your career with CEVA Air \& Ocean Italy S.P.A.
  
  

CEVA is committed to fair hiring procedures and to ensure that all employees have the opportunity to develop their potential. We want to ensure a work environment based on merit and inclusion, without any discrimination based on race, nationality, gender, sexual orientation, religious orientation or disability.
  
  

**Please note:** Legitimate CEVA Logistics recruitment processes include communication with candidates through recognized professional networks, such as LinkedIn or via an official company email address: firstname.lastname@cevalogistics.com. We recommend that you do not respond to unsolicited business propositions and/or offers from people with whom you are unfamiliar.

title: Enterprise IT Architect
description:
**Calling all innovators – find your future at Fiserv.**


We’re Fiserv, a global leader in Fintech and payments, and we move money and information in a way that moves the world. We connect financial institutions, corporations, merchants, and consumers to one another millions of times a day – quickly, reliably, and securely. Any time you swipe your credit card, pay through a mobile app, or withdraw money from the bank, we’re involved. If you want to make an impact on a global scale, come make a difference at Fiserv.

**Job Title**


Enterprise IT Architect**About your role:**


You will act as the technical authority and solution architect for Italian client card and payment systems while also serving as the front‑line technical lead for the client. This role blends strategic system design, architectural governance, and deep hands‑on support, ensuring all technical queries, incidents, and enhancements are handled effectively and with minimal business impact.


You will own the end‑to‑end technical landscape, guide design decisions, and lead complex problem resolution, while maintaining close day‑to‑day engagement with client stakeholders. This role is critical to ensuring platform resilience, regulatory compliance, and the continued success of a high‑revenue, mission‑critical client.

**What you’ll do:**

* Act as the overall technical architect for the VisionPLUS card processing platform and its integrations
* Serve as the primary senior technical contact for client, handling complex queries, escalations, and design discussions
* Lead major incident management, including deep‑dive diagnostics, cross‑team coordination, and root‑cause analysis. Provide hands‑on support for critical production issues across online and batch processing
* Design and govern distributed‑to‑mainframe integrations, including APIs and middleware. Review and approve technical changes, enhancements, and release plans to ensure system stability
* Mentor Advisor‑ and Engineer‑level team members, providing technical leadership and guidance
* Work closely with product owners, operations, infrastructure, and vendor teams to ensure seamless delivery
* Ensure systems meet 24×7 availability, performance, and security requirements
* Contribute to continuous improvement initiatives focused on resilience, automation, and technical debt reduction

**Experience you’ll need to have:**

* Extensive experience with VisionPLUS in a cards and payments processing environment
* Deep understanding of card lifecycle processing, including authorisation, clearing, settlement, billing, and disputes
* Strong hands‑on expertise with mainframe technologies, including: CICS (online transaction processing). DB2 (schema design, performance analysis, and data troubleshooting)
* Proven experience designing and supporting API‑based and event‑driven integrations with mainframe systems
* Strong background in production support, complex incident management, and root‑cause analysis in 24×7 environments
* Ability to balance architectural ownership with hands‑on execution when required
* Fluent Italian and English (spoken and written)
* Willingness to participate in out‑of‑hours (OOH) support for critical incidents

**Experience that would be great to have:**

* Experience acting as a solution or system architect for large‑scale payment platforms
* Strong knowledge of VisionPLUS modules and vendor roadmaps
* Familiarity with CICS tuning, DB2 optimisation, and batch/online workload balancing
* Knowledge of Swagger / OpenAPI standards and API governance
* Experience operating within ITIL‑aligned incident, problem, and change management frameworks
* Track record of supporting strategic, high‑revenue clients with strict SLA commitments
* Experience driving resilience, high availability, and disaster recovery strategies


Thank you for considering employment with Fiserv. Please:

* Apply using your legal name
* Complete the step\-by\-step profile and attach your resume (either is acceptable, both are preferable).

**Our commitment to Diversity and Inclusion:**


Fiserv is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, disability, protected veteran status, or any other category protected by law.

**Note to agencies:**


Fiserv does not accept resume submissions from agencies outside of existing agreements. Please do not send resumes to Fiserv associates. Fiserv is not responsible for any fees associated with unsolicited resume submissions.

**Warning about fake job posts:**


Please be aware of fraudulent job postings that are not affiliated with Fiserv. Fraudulent job postings may be used by cyber criminals to target your personally identifiable information and/or to steal money or financial information. Any communications from a Fiserv representative will come from a legitimate Fiserv email address.

Rotational shift nights/weekends**Environmental Conditions**


Cleanroom: no hair products, jewelry, makeup, nail polish, perfume, exposed piercings, facial hair etc... allowed**Job Description**


As part of the Thermo Fisher Scientific team, you’ll discover meaningful work that makes a positive impact on a global scale. Join our colleagues in bringing our Mission to life every single day to enable our customers to make the world healthier, cleaner and safer. We provide our global teams with the resources needed to achieve individual career goals while helping to take science a step beyond by developing solutions for some of the world’s toughest challenges, like protecting the environment, making sure our food is safe or helping find cures for cancer.  

  

DESCRIPTION:  

As a Team Leader in Manufacturing Operations at Thermo Fisher Scientific, you will guide and develop a high\-performing team while ensuring excellence in safety, quality, and production efficiency. You will coordinate department activities across shifts to meet production schedules and equipment efficiency requirements while maintaining strict adherence to cGMP standards. This role combines leadership with strategic operational oversight to deliver important products that help our customers make the world healthier, cleaner, and safer.  

  

Working closely with Quality, Engineering, Maintenance and other support functions, you will lead continuous improvement initiatives, optimize workflows, and cultivate a culture of safety and quality excellence. You will manage daily production activities, conduct performance reviews, provide coaching and training, and ensure compliance with all regulatory requirements while maintaining optimal cost\-benefit ratios.

* IOSH Managing Safely certification advantageous
* Lean Six Sigma certification (Yellow Belt or higher) beneficial
* Leadership experience managing teams of 5\-15 people
* Strong knowledge of cGMP, ISO standards and quality management systems
* Excellent troubleshooting and root cause analysis capabilities
* Advanced knowledge of manufacturing documentation and batch record requirements
* Proficiency with manufacturing execution systems and Microsoft Office Suite
* Strong communication and interpersonal skills for leading teams and interfacing with stakeholders

* Ability to work rotating shifts including nights, weekends and holidays as needed

* Capable of working in cleanroom environments with required PPE and gowning procedures

title: Junior Liquid R&D Scientist
description:
Oggi, Campari Group è un importante player nel settore degli spirits, con un portafoglio di fama mondiale di oltre 50 brand premium e super premium, commercializzati e distribuiti in più di 190 mercati in tutto il mondo, con posizioni leader in Europa e America.


Con sede principale a Milano, in Italia, Campari Group possiede **25** stabilimenti sparsi per il mondo e una rete di distribuzione in **26** Paesi, impiegando circa **4\.700** persone.


Le azioni della società capogruppo Davide Campari \- Milano N.V. sono quotate nella Borsa italiana dal 2001\. Oggi, Campari Group è il sesto più grande player al mondo nel settore degli spirits.

 **General Description of the Role**  

The Junior Liquid R\&D Scientist will support research and development (R\&D) initiatives within Aperitifs and other relevant product categories.  

**Key Responsibilities and Activities**  

* Support projects by batching new formulations and/or testing new processes.
* Support in ingredient qualifications with batching, trials, and testing.
* Support in renovation projects that may explore alternate formulations, ingredients, suppliers and/or processes.
* Support plants and/or comanufacturers on product and process optimization.
* Request and organize necessary technical and regulatory compliance documentation from suppliers.
* Execute accelerated stability testing and analyze results to predict shelf life and/or stability of products.
* Manage across multiple systems documentation control of all necessary ingredients, technical information, process directions, and formulations.
* Define and populate product specifications within a global centralized system.
* Input liquid bills of material within a global centralized system.
* Support in first production of products to ensure successful industrialization.
* Support pilot scale operations from blending, filtration, carbonation, bottling, and general maintenance.

  

**Key Relationships** ***Internal:***  

* Supervisors and Managers of Supply Chain
* Marketing
* Quality
* Regulatory
* Plant personnel

 ***External:***  

* Suppliers
* Co\-manufacturers
* Laboratories

 **Experience Required**
1\-2 years of product development and/or laboratory experience is preferred. **Education / Professional Qualifications**  

* Bachelor’s Degree in Food Science, Chemistry, Biology, Chemical Engineering, or other scientific discipline.

 ***Skills**** Team working (i.e. ability to work with others across all boundaries to reach common objectives).
* Good chemistry and mathematical skills are required.
* Sensory experience of foods and/or beverages is preferred.
* Good interpersonal, communication and organizational skills are required.
* Able to work within a lab environment and operate basic laboratory equipment.
* Data analysis experience is preferred.
* Competent in Microsoft Office Applications.
* Proficient in Italian and English.

  

  

**Questo è il nostro impegno nei confronti della diversità e dell'inclusione:**


In Campari Group crediamo nel creare più valore insieme, perciò consideriamo la diversità, in tutte le sue forme, una fonte di arricchimento. Le nostre politiche e pratiche occupazionali garantiscono il nostro impegno nell'offrire pari opportunità lavorative in tutti gli aspetti, indipendentemente da differenze di razza, religione, credo, nazionalità, origini, disabilità fisica, disabilità mentale, condizione medica, informazioni genetiche, stato civile, sesso, orientamento sessuale, identità, caratteristiche o espressione di genere, affiliazione o attività politica, età, stato di veterano/ex militare, cittadinanza o altra caratteristica protetta dalla legge.


Nota per il candidato:


La tua candidatura verrà valutata in base alle tue capacità, le tue competenze, le tue conoscenze generali e la tua esperienza, e non in base ad eventuali informazioni sensibili e proprietarie o a segreti commerciali di cui potresti essere a conoscenza. Non devi divulgare a Campari Group tali informazioni. Nel caso in cui ti venga posta una domanda alla quale non puoi rispondere senza divulgare informazioni riservate e proprietarie o segreti commerciali (inclusi quelli appartenenti a un datore di lavoro precedente o ai suoi fornitori o clienti), non devi rispondere alla domanda.


Il Gruppo Campari ritiene che l'equità retributiva e le pari opportunità siano una componente fondamentale per il benessere, l'empowerment e l'engagement dei dpendenti. I nostri sforzi per garantire equità retributiva ci hanno fatto ottenere la certificazione Fair Pay da parte di Fair Pay Workplace, un'organizzazione indipendente dedicata alla eliminazione delle disparità retributive basate su genere, razza e l'insieme delle condizioni.


Avviso per le agenzie terze:


Non accettiamo curricula non richiesti dai recruiters o dalle agenzie di collocamento. In assenza di un Contratto firmato, non considereremo e non accetteremo richieste di pagamento in caso di referral o recruiter fee. Nel caso in cui un recruiter terzo o un'agenzia inviino un curriculum o un candidato senza un accordo precedentemente firmato, ci riserviamo esplicitamente il diritto di cercare e assumere tali candidati senza alcun obbligo finanziario nei confronti del recruiter terzo o dell'agenzia.

title: Compliance Intern - Financial Crime Prevention
description:
**Responsibilities**
--------------------

**Mission:**


Our Company is seeking a motivated and detail\-oriented Compliance Intern to join the Compliance Department in Milan. This internship is focused on the review of higher\-sensitivity client dossiers (KYC) within a Corporate and Investment Banking environment. The position is offered for an initial duration of six (6\) months and may be extended to further 6 (six) months. Note: the internship is fully on\-site (no smart working) and is expected to cover the summer period.

**Tasks and responsibilities:**


Within the Compliance Department and under the supervision of senior Compliance Officers, the intern will support activities primarily related to Know Your Customer (KYC) and Anti\-Money Laundering (AML) controls, including:

* Reviewing and assessing risks related to new and existing clients (KYC), with a particular focus on higher\-risk and sensitive client profiles
* Performing detailed analysis of KYC files escalated to the Compliance function and ensuring completeness and regulatory adequacy
* Assessing business\-lines requests related to transactions or activities requiring enhanced scrutiny or specific approvals, in line with Bank of Italy AML red flags (indicatori di anomalia) and Suspicious Activity Reporting (SAR) obligations
* Verifying compliance with applicable KYC and AML regulations through regular examination of data, documentation, and internal systems
* Ensuring accurate and orderly maintenance of audit trails, including proper documentation of analyses, decisions, and escalations
* Collaborating closely with the financial security team in activities aimed at preventing money laundering, terrorist financing, and corruption


**Profile required**
--------------------

**Studies \& experience:**

* Bachelor’s degree in Law, Finance, Economics, Business Administration, or a related field
* Minimum of one (1\) year of prior experience in Know Your Customer (KYC) activities or client due diligence

**Language skills:**

* Fluency in both English and Italian (spoken and written)
* French is considered an asset

**Technical, operational \& Soft skills:**

* Good knowledge of Italian AML regulation and supervisory framework, as well as familiarity with EU AML/KYC requirements and international standards (e.g., EBA guidelines)
* Comfortable working with large volumes of documentation with a high level of accuracy and attention to detail
* Ability to manage administrative tasks diligently while meeting tight deadlines and performing effectively under pressure
* Strong interest in control, monitoring, and compliance activities
* Excellent Microsoft Suite skills, including data analysis and reporting and AI use (e.g. CoPilot Chat)
* Strong written and verbal communication skills


**Why join us**
---------------

Société Générale Italy is a multi\-category bank offering every employee the possibility to have several careers in a professional career path. Internal mobility and training are two core areas we focus on, in a dynamic, prosperous and evolving context.


As soon as you arrive, you will be integrated into our teams and will learn every day alongside our experts, who will support you in your tasks. Gradually, you will become more independent in your projects, making this experience a real career accelerator. You will also discover all the diversity of our businesses, in a sector that is constantly evolving and innovating.


At the end of your studies, various opportunities could be offered to you, in Italy and abroad


**Business insight**
--------------------

At Societe Generale, we are convinced that people are drivers of change, and that the world of tomorrow will be shaped by all their initiatives, from the smallest to the most ambitious.


Whether you’re joining us for a period of months, years or your entire career, together we can have a positive impact on the future. Creating, daring, innovating and taking action are part of our DNA.


If you too want to be directly involved, grow in a stimulating and caring environment, feel useful on a daily basis and develop or strengthen your expertise, you will feel right at home with us!


Still hesitating?


You should know that our employees can dedicate several days per year to solidarity actions during their working hours, including sponsoring people struggling with their orientation or professional integration, participating in the financial education of young apprentices, and sharing their skills with charities. There are many ways to get involved.


Diversity and Inclusion
We are an **equal opportunities employer** and we are proud to make diversity a strength for our company. Societe Generale is committed to recognizing and **promoting all talents**, regardless of their beliefs, age, disability, parental status, ethnic origin, nationality, gender identity, sexual orientation, membership of a political, religious, trade union or minority organisation, or any other characteristic that could be subject to discrimination.

title: Data Labeling Specialist
description:
**Apply here:** **https://operationsarmy.com/application**
----------------------------------------------------------

**Job Summary**
---------------


We are seeking detail\-oriented and reliable **Data Labeling Specialists** to support our AI and machine learning initiatives, with a strong focus on **audio transcription, annotation, and voice data collection**.


You will play a critical role in training AI systems by labeling and structuring data with high accuracy. High\-performing candidates may grow into **Reviewer (QA)** roles, helping ensure quality and consistency across datasets.

**Languages Needed**
--------------------


Italian

**Key Responsibilities**
------------------------

* Label and annotate audio, text, image, or video data
* Transcribe audio with speaker labels and timestamps
* Maintain high accuracy and consistency
* Follow structured annotation guidelines
* Flag unclear or low\-quality data
* Meet productivity and accuracy targets

### **Reviewer / QA (if applicable)**

* Review labeled outputs for quality
* Provide feedback and identify error trends
* Support team calibration and training

**Skills \& Competencies**
--------------------------

* Audio transcription \& listening accuracy
* Data annotation \& labeling
* Attention to detail \& consistency
* Time and task management
* Captcha handling
* Boolean search (basic)
* File and data organization

**Requirements**
----------------

* Fluency in at least one required language
* Strong listening and comprehension skills
* Ability to follow complex instructions
* Basic computer skills
* Ability to work independently

**Apply here:** **https://operationsarmy.com/application**
